author: @BrentLeVasseur
text: Radiant energy are longitudinal/scalar waves in the aether/zpe field. They are produced with high voltage impulses sent through a bifilar wound coil, where by the two transverse electro-magnetic waves collapse at 180 degrees phase difference to produce a single longitudinal standing wave. Maxwell’s original equations in their quaternion form had scalar/longitudinal waves represented, but they were eliminated by Heaviside when he over simplified them into today’s formulations which excluded the longitudinal/scalar waves, which is what Tesla called “radiant energy”.

author: @designsforge4153
text:
Imagine that gravity operates similarly to an electric charge system. When you're grounded, your charge is neutral. As you jump, you gather an opposite charge, causing the ground to pull you back down. The higher you go, the more opposite charge you accumulate. Objects with larger surface areas gather more of this charge, limiting their jumping ability. If you manage to attain the same charge as Earth, you might achieve levitation. This theory suggests gravity could be influenced by electrostatic forces, and manipulating charge could impact gravitational effects.

Dont mind, I am a creative artist :P

Essentially, tesla discovered that ionising radiation can cause a conductive plate to gain a charge through surface ionisation and invented a very simple, very rudementary wideband solar cell.

Radiant energy is just EMF.

author: @kokeskokeskokes
text:
What I don't think is happening is what Tesla proposed: That it is caused by special positively charged minuscule particles travelling at 50c and passing through planets like nothing. In such case we would not see a dramatic change with altitude.
What I do think is that you are tapping atmospheric electricity. The reason behind is that we already know that ionosphere contains all molecules dissociated into ions. Perhaps these ions travel down and disappear so gradually, that even at sea level some exist.
When we apply electric current, what happens it that large body of Earth releases electrons. With "radiant energy generator" we simplify the transfer of said electrons from Earth to ions in the atmosphere, like a tiny lightening bolt. The device looks like one plate of a capacitor to me, second plate being the atmosphere itself.
Thank you for another excellent video.

author: @Electrogravityphysics
text:
Robert Murray-Smith, interesting experiment.  
As an electronics engineer, the first thing I would do is to investigate the nature of the voltage on your plates.  The easiest way to do this is be to hook up a high bandwidth scope (100 MHz) to see the waveform and dominant frequency of your AC signal.  Then we can discuss if you are picking up random noise, the local radio station - or something else.
Let me know if you have any questions.
-Nils

replies.author: @DeliciousDeBlair
replies.text:
If I am going to go to the trouble of making one of these its going to be on a scale with realistic harvestable power. The only way to do that is to get high enough up to produce a voltage one can 'turn down' and still have enough amperage to make it usable.

This means a weather balloon, not a party balloon.

Interestingly, one of the best potential tethers for such a process is a carbon fiber type material because of its mass to strength ratio. Another is spider silk, which could be lightly coated in a conductive carbon.

As long as the ohms are not too terribly high, and the volts are sufficiently high, one could realistically collect a usable amount of power from heights below that of typical flight paths of aircraft for localized use.

With the addition of some form of super low mass condenser [I was thinking aluminized Mylar sheet or even 'christmas tree tinsel'] one can in theory collect a lot of ionic particle energy so long as this does not lead to the balloon growing too heavy to remain in the air.

My ultimate scheme includes a self regulating helium flow control which at one and the same time compensates for changes in heat, altitude, gross mass and weather hazards.

If the balloon were built in such a manner it could deflate and reel its self in during harsh weather its rate of survivability would be far higher.

To be able to adjust for wind speed, wind direction, heating [or cooling] of the helium, leakage through the shell, gain and loss of dust and moisture, etc, one can have a balloon which is always at a desired height except when storms and similar events prohibit, allowing for even remote tethering, and therefore wide area scavenging of ions and EM radiation simultaneously.

The size of the balloon is not a fixed or arbitrary volume, but it would have to be able to hold aloft what ever collectors one used as well as its own tether [the mass of the tether is a variable too] and the effects of rain, snow, fog, dew, dust, birds, etc.

The tether could have to hold against winds in excess of 100 knots at least long enough to pull the balloon in to safety so it would not be just a string, but to be light it would have to be something pretty high tensile compared to its mass, and if its self regulating, it has to be at least partially gas tight to deliver helium to the balloon at the top, as well as possibly microfilament command wires to send impulses up the line to the balloon to control venting of gas.

author: @lisakingscott7729
text:
Isn't this just an untuned and undected radio receiver? The circuit is like a crystal receiver minus the coil and minus the diode. The coil would normally form a tuned circuit with the capacitor and select the frequency you want and the diode acts as the detector, half wave rectifying the signal so that it can be heard (with low pass filtering intrinsic in the headphones). Without the coil and diode, the AC meter will be indicating a wide band of RF, bandwidth limited by the frequency response of the meter. The capacitor probably limits the bandwidth to a certain extent and may not be necessary. A similar act of your hand touching the 'antenna', with and without gloves to improve the signal, can be demonstrated on an FM radio with the telescopic aerial in the closed position. When you touch the aerial, a stronger signal will be heard. Placing a thin insulator between will not affect the signal improvement much, the signal passing through the thin insulator, as if it were a series capacitor.
So, the energy you are seeing is probably wideband radio energy from a wide range of sources.

text: To somewhat explain your results there is always charges in the air as we clearly see in thunderstorms. Thous same charges are always present just not with high enough energy to generate lightning. So there is a certain charge pr meter of hight potential from the ground. Thou you will never get any significant current from it, but you can get hundreds and thousands of volts if its set up right. And the thing about the spark gap is so you can use it as an oscillator into a coil.

author: @DanaGould0
text:
Maybe I'm wrong, but at 2:10 you showed how the meter jumps up when you touch it. Then at 2:40 you're holding over your head (and of course touching it) and the meter jumps up to barely 1/10 volt more than when you're touching it at 2:10. I'm not sure that demonstrates much.

I think you need to put it on an elevated insulated stand of some kind to get a good measurement.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are quite right it doesn't demonstrate a big jump but it does demonstrate a jump - you are equally right it needs to be higher and insulated.

author: @VICTORIA-SAYERS
text: P.S. Based on how it drops down when you place the Plates on the Floor I'm guessing it's collecting and storing Charge (which is why I thought it acted like a Capacitor) from both the Air as well as from any other Electrically active potential (your Hands ect) because the Floor is usually well Insulated. Have you tested the Heat difference on the Ceiling as to on the Floor? If the reading on the Ceiling is not much different from on the Floor then that's pointing to Electrical excitement in both the higher altitudes as well as from Charged surfaces like your Hands ect, but if the Ceiling is much warmer than your Hands then that could explain the increase in the Meter.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good points mate - i'll have a look at that

replies.author: @VICTORIA-SAYERS
replies.text: Your welcome, I would check the temp on the outside of the Plates and in between and I would also check the temps on both the Ceiling and Floor to use as a cross reference to judge if it's just the Heat as opposed to being a combination of both Heat and the Electrical Charge in the Air and your Hands. Great work!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate - i am going to put it on the back burner for a little while to finish the swag obviously but i will look through all the comments before i pick it up again

replies.author: @VICTORIA-SAYERS
replies.text: I see, okay, awesome work, look forward to more Vids!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
*****
too much to do and not enough time to do it all mate - i am hoping others will pick up on this and run with it

author: @VAXKILLEDMYDAD
text: Another excellent and fascinating demo!  I just wanna check, because I'm not completely sure. The copper strips on your plastic sheets are on the reverse side of the ink layer, right? So there's no connection between the two? Have you used typical OHP sheets, or something else?  Thanks in advance mate. Such a simple set up!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Hiya Mate, no it's just the plates for the SWAG - I was asked to do this as a quick test the construction details are in the SWAG generator prototype update video it is just ohp sheets painted with the ink - the copper strips are under the ink and act as current collectors - so you put the strips on the plastic, paint them over and you are done - but as i say details are in the swag vids

replies.author: @VAXKILLEDMYDAD
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Cheers mate!  I've watched them all, but I get the wrong end of the stick sometimes. I will get down the shops tomorrow and get the OHP sheets, and give it a go.

author: @consciousenergies
text: There is potential in all things, for all things are energy. In transition from one state to another, it is possible to influence higher vibrational energies in a resonant conductive medium. I have done this same experiment as described in the patent.  The difference here is that you have a conductive medium that allows a more robust set of energetic frequencies. Like using a smaller string on a guitar,  its apt to move with ever so slight amounts of potential energy exerted. It's vibrational resonance is a tune to a higher frequency. All hypothesis I guess, but from some experiments I have studied and conducted, it seems nature gives subtle hints in reoccurring patterns. Thanks as always, looking forward to experimenting with this ink. :)﻿

text:
This is most interesting as ever, Your endeavors spark the imagination for which I thank you
I would like to second the comment "directeeccks" made below.  Energy storage is thing I have been most interested in, not that generation is not important but here we enjoy super warm summers with loads of energy then cold cold winters.....oh to have some of that summer energy in the cellar to use.
Any thoughts on stable storage other than the vanadium flow cell would be of great interest.
.

author: @directeeccks
text: Man I wish I was close enough to help with testing.  There are too many huge applications coming from this channel.  As always I am thinking of how this could effect the world if the results are controllable, repeatable, and usable.  Then the only thing that would be holding us back is storage of the energy.  Which could very well be a product of graphene itself.  Amazing find.   Cheers!

author: @ArkiverUnifiedEnergy
text: Wow! Cool! Thanks mate. Your videos always have me thinking on the subject. Through out the video it looks like the voltage is hitting 7 (2:51 7.196volts) with quick movement. LOL! That's amazing. I think there are several things going on. Elevation, capacitance and function as an antenna. Ionized air flowing over the plates like seawater. Light also resonating from plate to plate as it passes through. It could also function as a static charge or ozone converter. A motionless unit windmill might be possible. Your ink opens some amazing possibilities. I look forward to seeing what the future holds.

author: @jfausset
text: Not that I don't think this is cool, but I want to point out performance. The cell, at its best, using you as a capacitor or a heat source, performed at about .04 W per square foot. Your thermocell performed at about 0.25 W per square foot.  Both were at ambient (roughly STP).  Do you believe one to be more promising than the other?   I see that not knowing how either really works makes that difficult, but right now at a glance the thermocell looks better.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: hiya mate - at this stage i just think it is interesting - i will carry on with the swag - but other know more about this than me and it is here to show what is possible and just that it was interesting

author: @fabrizioricciarelli
text:
Robert, you rock! As I can see in the video, the SWAG acts as
- antenna when you put it in the air (collecting the energy from the aerial waves, transversal or longitudinal ones);
- as a "Alessandro Volta" battery when you put some salt water on it (chemical reaction)...
It seems that these characteristics are typical of a carbon-based material.
I think that air pressure (elevation-dependant), temperature and/or moving magnetic fields can affect the power produced by the cell too.
As you perfectly know, the most important thing is the power (Watts), not only volts: could you please show/tell us how many of them can the cell produce in their different situations (aerial, salt water, pressure, magnetic, etc.)? I love your work, go ahead and thank you for sharing your precious research.

author: @deslomeslager
text:
I have a cordless phone (can transmit power). I have got WIFI (and all of my neighbors as well). My mobile will once and a while connect to the GSM network. And My office room is a kind of Faraday Cage (I can only receive a very local FM station, not much more). If I touch the outside of my radio (plastic) I hear a loud noise. And I found out one of the other transmitters in my office is the power adapter for my Laptop (transmits a terrible lot of noise). And I have a inverter for my solar panels in the same room (seems to give noise as well on a lot of apparatus). So where does the power come from? Needs some research. Is your WiFi transmitter is mounted rather high in the workplace?

Also note, the ground should be considered as a source. No need to say the ground is relative.

And from what I understood of the radiant energy Tesla had, it seems to me that electrons were excited on the collector plate because of what was radiated upon it.

Did you try and attach just a piece of metal, and hold it up? It might generate almost the same (who knows?)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good point i'll do the metal test - cheers mate
